skill.name: arxiv-database
skill.description: Search and retrieve scientific preprints from arXiv by keyword, author, or category. Fetches paper metadata including abstract, DOI, and PDF URL. Can download PDFs for offline reading.
skill.summary: Query the arXiv repository to find scientific papers and download their metadata and PDFs programmatically.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 90 static analysis findings are false positives. The high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags (9 locations) correspond to license fields, JSON metadata keys, and argparse setup lines that have no cryptographic code. The 74 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' flags are markdown code formatting in documentation files, not executable code. The 6 'hardcoded URL' flags are legitimate references to arXiv API documentation and the skill's GitHub repository. The single 'Python os file operations' flag at scripts/arxiv_search.py:51 (os.makedirs for PDF download directory) is expected, bounded functionality. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or credential theft patterns were found. The skill is a legitimate arXiv search and download tool with minimal residual risk.

high_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm
high_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: All 9 flagged locations contain no cryptographic code. Flagged lines include SKILL.md metadata (license: MIT), arxiv-database_audit_result_v1.json field names (e.g. 'security' key), and scripts/arxiv_search.py argparse setup. These are text data and configuration lines, not cryptographic operations.

medium_findings.title: Ruby/shell backtick execution
medium_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: All 74 flagged locations use markdown backtick formatting for documentation (code spans and bash code blocks). Locations in references/api_docs.md are markdown syntax prefixes like `ti`, `au`, `abs`. Locations in SKILL.md are bash code block examples showing python command usage. No Ruby backtick shell execution is present.

medium_findings.title: Python os file operations
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE but LOW risk: os.makedirs(args.dir) at scripts/arxiv_search.py:51 creates a directory for PDF downloads. The directory path is user-specified via --dir argument (default: '.'). This is expected, bounded functionality for a file download tool. No path traversal risk beyond what the user already controls.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Flagged lines in arxiv-database_audit_result_v1.json are evaluation metadata notes describing test scope ('practice boundaries held', 'scope remained controlled'). These are assessment documentation, not reconnaissance commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: URLs in references/api_docs.md point to the official arXiv API documentation (arxiv.org/help/api/user-manual) and the legitimate arxiv Python library (github.com/lukasschwab/arxiv.py). The URL in SKILL.md points to the skill's own GitHub source repository (github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). All URLs are legitimate references supporting the skill's documented functionality.

content.user_title: Search and retrieve papers from the arXiv repository
content.value_statement: Finding relevant scientific papers from arXiv requires navigating multiple web searches and manual metadata collection. This skill automates arXiv searches by keyword, author, or category, returning structured metadata and optionally downloading PDFs.
actual_capabilities: Search arXiv by keyword, author name, category prefix (e.g. cs.AI), or arXiv ID
Retrieve paper metadata including title, authors, publication date, abstract, and PDF URL
Sort results by relevance, submission date, or last updated date
Limit search results to a configurable maximum number
Download PDF files of search results to a specified local directory
Combine search filters with boolean operators (AND, OR, ANDNOT)
limitations: Cannot search PubMed, Google Scholar, or other databases outside arXiv
Does not extract or summarize paper content beyond the provided abstract
PDF downloads depend on arXiv availability and may fail for older papers
Search results are limited to what the arXiv API returns (typically max 300 results per query)

use_cases.title: Literature review for research papers
use_cases.description: A graduate student searches for the latest papers on quantum computing and collects metadata for their literature review section.

use_cases.title: Tracking new publications in a field
use_cases.description: A research scientist monitors new submissions in machine learning by searching cs.LG sorted by submission date each week.

use_cases.title: Building a paper reference database
use_cases.description: A technical writer collects papers on a specific topic and downloads their PDFs into a structured directory for offline reference.

prompt_templates.title: Simple keyword search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search arXiv for papers about [topic keyword] and show me the title, authors, and abstract for each result.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick look-up of papers on a specific topic

prompt_templates.title: Category-filtered search with sort
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the 10 most recent papers in category [cs.AI/cs.CL/cs.LG] about [subtopic], sorted by submission date. Show me the title, authors, date, and PDF URL for each.
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted search within a specific arXiv category

prompt_templates.title: Author-based paper discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Search arXiv for papers by author [author name] from the last year. Show the title, publication date, and abstract for each paper found.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding all recent publications by a specific researcher

prompt_templates.title: Batch download for offline reading
prompt_templates.prompt: Search arXiv for papers about [topic] in category [category], limit to 5 results sorted by relevance. Download the PDFs to a folder called ./papers and confirm each download with the file path.
prompt_templates.scenario: Collecting papers for offline reading or batch processing

output_examples.input: Search arXiv for papers about reinforcement learning in cs.AI
output_examples.output: Found 5 papers in category cs.AI about reinforcement learning. For each paper, the skill returns: title, list of authors, publication date, PDF URL link, and a 200-character preview of the abstract.

output_examples.input: Download the latest 3 papers on diffusion models to a local folder
output_examples.output: The skill runs the arXiv search for diffusion models in the requested category, displays metadata for each of the 3 papers found, downloads each PDF to the specified directory, and prints the file path of each downloaded PDF.

best_practices: Use category prefixes (cat:cs.AI, cat:cs.CL) in your query to narrow results to relevant subfields
Set --max-results to a small number first to test your query before running large searches
Specify a dedicated --dir path when downloading PDFs to keep files organized
anti_patterns: Do not use very short single-word queries like 'AI' which return thousands of unsorted results
Do not download PDFs without first reviewing search results to confirm paper relevance
Do not rely on this skill for systematic reviews requiring database-specific query syntax like MeSH terms

faq.question: What is arXiv?
faq.answer: arXiv is an open-access repository for scientific preprints in fields like physics, mathematics, computer science, and biology. This skill searches that repository.

faq.question: Does this skill work with PubMed or Google Scholar?
faq.answer: No. This skill only searches the arXiv repository. It cannot query PubMed, Google Scholar, IEEE, or other databases.

faq.question: Can this skill download full paper PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --download flag when running the script. PDFs are saved to the directory you specify with --dir.

faq.question: What categories are available for searching?
faq.answer: arXiv supports many categories including cs.AI (Artificial Intelligence), cs.CL (Computation and Language), cs.CV (Computer Vision), cs.LG (Machine Learning), and physics subcategories.

faq.question: How many results can I get from one search?
faq.answer: You can set --max-results to any number. The arXiv API typically returns up to 300 results per query. Use specific queries to keep results relevant.

faq.question: Does this skill require Python packages?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill uses the `arxiv` Python package (version 1.4.0 or later). Install it with: pip install arxiv>=1.4.0
